PREFERREDLABEL: caring for adults who are not sick
ALTLABELS:
caring for adults who are not sick
helping adults in another house or family
affective/emotional support for non-dependent adult household and non-family members
emotional support for non-family members
offering encouragement and support
feeding, cleaning, physical care for non-dependent adult household and non-family members including for temporary illness
DESCRIPTION:
Refers to the provision of help to non-dependent adults from other households and non-family members.

Examples:
- Cheering up non-dependent adults from other households
- Talking and listening to non-dependent adults who are not family members
- Took care of a neighbour’s (non-dependent) mother with flu
- Giving personal care to temporarily sick adults from another household
- Giving medical/health-care to temporarily sick adults who are not part of one’s own household or family
- Other specified physical care of temporarily sick non-dependent adults from other households

SCOPENOTE:
Includes unpaid volunteer activities such as:
- Production of goods as help
- Provision of services as help
- Purchasing inputs as help
- Disposing or selling outputs as help
ALTLABELS:
helping
helping in other people's businesses without getting paid
unpaid volunteer unpaid help in enterprises owned by other households
unpaid assistance in household enterprises
Assistance
DESCRIPTION:
Refers to unpaid, non-compulsory activities to help in enterprises owned by other households as help to other households, not arranged by an organization.

Examples:
- Help my neighbour in his business

SCOPENOTE:
Includes unpaid volunteer activities such as:
- Preparing correspondence, assistance repairs and other odd jobs for the organization as help
- Giving information, distributing leaflets as help
- Work as committee member, fund-raising activities as help
- Participation in civic, professional, fraternal, political etc. organizations as help

Refers to unpaid, non-compulsory activities to undertake office/administrative work as help, arranged by the community or an organization.
